title: Remove the orphaned legacy config directory ~/.config/herdr-gateway
action: Delete the directory ~/.config/herdr-gateway (outside the repo, in the user's XDG config home). It is the pre-rename legacy config location for herdr-go, superseded by ~/.config/herdr-go, which install.sh/dev-deploy.sh already treat as canonical (they only migrate the legacy dir into the canonical one when the canonical one does not yet exist, and otherwise leave the legacy one untouched with a warning). Verified before deletion: ~/.config/herdr-gateway/config.json is byte-identical to ~/.config/herdr-go/config.json (diff produced no output), ~/.config/herdr-gateway/herdctl.env holds an old web secret no longer referenced (the running herdr-go-dev.service reads its secret from ~/.config/herdr-go/herdr-go.env instead, confirmed via the active systemd unit's EnvironmentFile), and there is no legacy data directory (~/.local/share/herdr-gateway does not exist). Nothing in the repo or the installers reads ~/.config/herdr-gateway once the canonical directory exists.

truths: ~/.config/herdr-go (canonical, in active use by the running herdr-go-dev.service) is untouched
prohibitions: Do not stop or restart the running herdr-go-dev.service

trace.outcome: Deleted the orphaned legacy config dir ~/.config/herdr-gateway; canonical ~/.config/herdr-go and the running herdr-go-dev.service were untouched
